title: 10 best herbal teas for gum swelling
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum swelling are natural remedies made from various plants and herbs that can help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated gums. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to alleviate gum swelling and promote oral health. Examples of herbal teas that can help with gum swelling include chamomile, calendula, and clove teas. Chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling and irritation, while calendula tea has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help promote gum healing. Clove tea contains eugenol, a natural anesthetic that can help alleviate gum pain and reduce inflammation. By using herbal teas for gum swelling, individuals can find relief from discomfort and improve their oral health, leading to an overall improvement in their quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roman chamomile tea helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can reduce inflammation and fight bacteria in the mouth. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe irritated gums, while its antiseptic properties help to prevent infection. Chamomile tea also contains flavonoids, which are known to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. Drinking chamomile tea or rinsing the mouth with chamomile tea can help to alleviate gum swelling and promote oral health. Additionally, chamomile tea has a calming effect, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, contributing factors to gum inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roman chamomile tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol, helps reduce gum swelling by decreasing inflammation.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of Roman chamomile tea, attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids, is important for gum swelling as it inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria causing gum infections.
Analgesic: Roman chamomile tea's analgesic effect, owing to its volatile oils and flavonoids, is crucial for gum swelling as it alleviates pain and discomfort associated with inflamed gums.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of Roman chamomile tea, primarily due to its phenolic compounds, is significant for gum swelling as it combats oxidative stress and promotes tissue healing.

Circulation-enhancing: Roman chamomile tea's circulation-enhancing property, attributed to its flavonoids and essential oils, is vital for gum swelling as it improves blood flow, facilitating nutrient and oxygen supply to the gum tissues, thus promoting recovery.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle.
Place one teaspoon of dried Roman chamomile flowers in a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the chamomile flowers.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and enjoy the warm tea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, chamomile flowers help reduce gum swelling and provide pain relief.
Leaves: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile leaves aid in reducing inflammation and soothing irritated gums.
Stems: With antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile stems contribute to reducing gum swelling and fighting infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with gum swelling because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria that can cause gum disease and swelling. Additionally, sage tea has astringent properties that can help tighten gum tissue and reduce bleeding. Drinking sage tea or using it as a mouthwash can help soothe irritated gums and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea reduces gum swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic compounds.

Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, thus preventing gum infections that can cause swelling, due to the antibacterial properties of camphor, camphene, and 1,8-cineole.

Antioxidant: Sage tea promotes gum health by neutralizing free radicals and protecting gum tissues from oxidative stress, owing to its rich antioxidant content, including rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.
Astringent: It helps tighten gum tissues and reduce swelling by causing a contraction of the proteins in the gums, thanks to the tannins present in sage.
Analgesic: Sage tea alleviates gum pain and discomfort associated with swelling, as it contains pain-relieving compounds such as α- and β-thujone.
remedy_recipe: Boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh sage leaves to a teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the sage leaves in the teapot.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes.
Strain and drink the sage tea, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, sage leaves help reduce gum swelling.
Flowers: Containing soothing properties, sage flowers can help alleviate irritation and swelling in the gums.
Bark: With high levels of antioxidants, sage bark can help decrease gum inflammation and promote healing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tea helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight against infection. The rosmarinic acid in rosemary can inhibit the growth of bacteria, which can cause gum disease and lead to gum swelling. Additionally, rosemary is rich in antioxidants that can help to promote healing and reduce redness and irritation in the gums. Drinking rosemary tea or using it as a mouth rinse can help to soothe and relieve gum swelling, providing a natural and effective solution for maintaining good oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tea's anti-inflammatory property reduces gum swelling by decreasing inflammation and irritation in the gums, thanks to the presence of active constituents like rosmarinic acid and carnosol.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property helps control gum swelling by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum inflammation and infections, attributable to compounds like eucalyptol and α-pinene.

Analgesic: Rosemary tea's analgesic effect alleviates gum swelling by numbing the pain and discomfort caused by inflammation, with active constituents such as caffeic acid and rosemarinic acid contributing to this property.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of rosemary tea helps reduce gum swelling by neutralizing free radicals and promoting tissue repair, mainly due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and carnosol.

Circulation-enhancing: Rosemary tea's circulation-enhancing property improves gum swelling by increasing blood flow to the gums, which aids in healing and reducing inflammation, with active constituents like rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid playing a crucial role.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried rosemary leaves or 2 teaspoons of fresh rosemary leaves to a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the rosemary leaves and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and gently press the leaves to release any remaining liquid.
Allow the tea to cool down before drinking, or store it in the refrigerator for later use.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of rosemary are often used in medicinal teas for gum swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Rosemary flowers contain high levels of antioxidants, making them useful for reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums.
Stems: The stems of rosemary contain essential oils that have antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent infections in the gums.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for gum swelling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with gum swelling because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. Thyme is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have a protective effect on gum tissue. Drinking thyme tea or using it as a mouthwash can help soothe irritated gums and promote oral health. Additionally, thyme tea contains high levels of antioxidants that can help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the gums. Overall, thyme tea's unique combination of anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties make it an effective natural remedy for gum swelling.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea reduces gum swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of active compounds like carvacrol and thymol.
Antibacterial: It inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria responsible for gum inflammation, as thymol and other volatile oils in thyme exhibit strong antibacterial properties.
Antioxidant: Thyme tea's high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and promotes healing.
Analgesic: Thyme tea alleviates gum swelling pain by acting as a natural analgesic, with active constituents like thymol and carvacrol providing soothing relief.
Circulation-boosting: Thyme tea enhances blood circulation in the gums, facilitating the delivery of nutrients and immune cells to inflamed areas, which aids in gum swelling reduction.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried thyme or 2 tablespoons of fresh thyme leaves.
Steep - Allow the thyme to steep in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain - Strain the liquid to remove the thyme leaves.
Sweeten - Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Serve - Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature before drinking.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce gum swelling.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help soothe irritated gums and reduce inflammation.
Stems: Thyme stems have astringent properties that can help tighten gum tissue, reducing swelling and promoting healing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the gums. The tea is also rich in antioxidants that help to promote gum health and healing. Furthermore, licorice tea has antibacterial properties that can help to fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth that can cause gum inflammation. Drinking licorice tea can provide relief from gum swelling and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Licorice tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to its triterpenoid saponins like glycyrrhizin, helps reduce gum swelling by decreasing inflammation.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial effect of licorice tea, caused by its flavonoids and coumarins, is crucial for gum swelling as it combats the bacterial infections causing gum inflammation.
Antioxidant: Licorice tea's antioxidant property, derived from its phenolic compounds, is important for gum swelling as it counters oxidative stress and promotes tissue healing.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of licorice tea, owing to its flavonoids and triterpenoids, is vital for gum swelling as it alleviates pain and discomfort.

Immune-modulatory: Licorice tea's immune-modulatory property, attributed to its glycyrrhizin, is significant for gum swelling as it helps regulate the immune response and assists in managing gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, place one cup of water and two slices of dried licorice root.
Boil the mixture for 10 minutes on medium heat.
Turn off the heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove licorice root slices.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce gum swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various oral health issues, including gum swelling, due to their soothing and healing effects.
Seeds: Licorice seeds contain flavonoids that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them useful for treating gum swelling and pain.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains compounds that can help reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, making it a potential remedy for gum swelling and other oral health issues.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elderberry tea helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help to boost the immune system and promote oral health. Additionally, elderberry tea has antibacterial properties that can help to fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth that can cause gum inflammation. Drinking elderberry tea regularly may help to soothe and heal swollen gums, promoting better oral health and hygiene.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Elderberry tea contains compounds such as flavonoids and anthocyanins that help reduce inflammation, which is important for gum swelling as it alleviates pain and promotes healing.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in elderberry tea, including vitamin C, aids in tissue repair and protection from further damage, contributing to gum health.

Antimicrobial: Elderberry tea has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of compounds like anthocyanins and flavonoids, which inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria that can cause gum infections and worsen swelling.
Analgesic: Elderberry tea has natural pain-relieving properties, which help alleviate discomfort caused by gum swelling, making it easier to maintain oral hygiene.
Immune system support: Elderberry tea is known to boost the immune system, helping the body fight off infections and inflammation that can lead to gum swelling and other oral health issues.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried elderberries.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the solid elderberry remnants.
Add honey to taste, stirring until fully dissolved.
Sip the warm tea, allowing it to slowly soothe your gums.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Elderberry flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for reducing gum swelling.
Bark: The bark of elderberry contains tannins that have astringent properties, helping to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums.
Leaves: Elderberry leaves contain various beneficial compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for treating gum swelling.
Berries: Elderberry berries are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them helpful in reducing gum swelling and promoting oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with gum swelling because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery and thick when it comes into contact with water. When you drink marshmallow tea, the mucilage coats your gums and forms a protective barrier. This barrier can help to reduce inflammation and irritation, which can help to alleviate gum swelling. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can further help to reduce gum swelling and promote oral health. The soothing and calming effects of marshmallow tea can also help to reduce stress and tension, which can contribute to gum health issues. Drinking marshmallow tea regularly can help to maintain healthy gums and prevent gum swelling.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage that forms a protective film on the gums, reducing pain and inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The tea's flavonoids and phenolic acids help reduce gum swelling by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals.
Antibacterial property: Marshmallow tea's antimicrobial compounds, such as tannins and phenolic acids, help control gum inflammation by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria.
Analgesic property: The tea's rosmarinic acid and other constituents help alleviate gum pain and discomfort associated with swelling.
Emollient property: Marshmallow tea's mucilage provides a soothing effect on gums, promoting tissue repair and healing.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the solids.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and stir until dissolved.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory properties, marshmallow leaves are used to soothe gum swelling and irritation.
Flowers: Packed with mucilage, marshmallow flowers help reduce inflammation and promote gum tissue healing.
Roots: Containing high levels of mucilage and antioxidants, marshmallow roots are beneficial for treating gum swelling and providing relief.
Bark: With demulcent properties, marshmallow bark aids in reducing gum inflammation and providing a protective coating on the swollen area.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet violet tea helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the gums. The tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the gums from damage and promote healing. Additionally, sweet violet tea has antibacterial properties that can help to eliminate harmful bacteria in the mouth that can cause gum swelling and other oral health problems. Drinking sweet violet tea can also help to soothe and calm inflamed gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sweet violet tea helps reduce gum swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and salicylates.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of sweet violet tea combat harmful bacteria that cause gum inflammation and swelling, due to the action of compounds like saponins and tannins.
Analgesic: Sweet violet tea alleviates gum swelling pain by numbing the affected area, owing to the presence of pain-relieving constituents such as salicylates.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of sweet violet tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing swelling.
Astringent: Sweet violet tea's astringent properties tighten and constrict gum tissues, reducing swelling and promoting healing, due to the presence of tannins.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried sweet violet flowers.
Crush the flowers gently to release their oils.
Steep the crushed flowers in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid, discarding the spent flowers.
Sip the warm tea slowly, allowing it to soothe gum swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective for soothing gum swelling.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, beneficial for reducing gum inflammation.
Roots: Historically used in traditional medicine to treat gum disorders, thanks to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with gum swelling because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's natural compounds work to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated gums. Additionally, the tea's antibacterial properties help to eliminate harmful bacteria that can cause gum disease and lead to gum swelling. Drinking large-leaved lime tea regularly can help to maintain healthy gums and prevent gum swelling. The tea's natural ingredients provide a gentle and effective way to alleviate gum discomfort and promote oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Large-leaved lime tea contains flavonoids and tannins that help reduce gum swelling by decreasing inflammation.
Antibacterial: The presence of antibacterial agents such as limonene and alpha-pinene in large-leaved lime tea inhibits the growth of bacteria causing gum inflammation.
Antioxidant: Large-leaved lime tea is rich in vitamin C and flavonoids that exhibit antioxidant properties, aiding in tissue repair and reducing gum swelling.
Astringent: The astringent properties of large-leaved lime tea, due to its tannin content, help tighten and heal gum tissues, reducing swelling.
Analgesic: Large-leaved lime tea contains compounds like citral and linalool with analgesic properties, helping alleviate gum swelling and pain.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried lime leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of crushed lime bark and 1 tablespoon of dried peppermint leaves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 tablespoon of dried large-leaved lime flowers, and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea, then drink it warm, up to 3 times a day, as needed for gum swelling relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and tannins, the leaves of the large-leaved lime plant are often used to make medicinal teas for gum swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Flowers: Containing essential oils and antioxidants, the flowers of the large-leaved lime plant are used in teas for gum swelling to help reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Bark: The bark of the large-leaved lime plant is a source of beneficial compounds, such as terpenes and phenolic acids, making it suitable for use in medicinal teas that help alleviate gum swelling and pain.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and vitamins, the seeds of the large-leaved lime plant can be used in teas for gum swelling to provide soothing relief and support overall oral health.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Littleleaf linden tea helps with gum swelling because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums. The tea is made from the flowers and leaves of the linden tree, which contain flavonoids and volatile oils that have a soothing effect on the gums. Drinking linden tea or rinsing the mouth with it can help alleviate gum discomfort and promote oral health. The tea's anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties can also help prevent gum disease and other oral infections. Furthermore, linden tea has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, contributing factors to gum problems.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Littleleaf linden tea helps reduce gum swelling by decreasing inflammation, thanks to its active constituents, including flavonoids and volatile oils.
Antioxidant: Its high antioxidant content, such as phenolic acids and tannins, promotes gum healing and reduces swelling by protecting gum tissues from oxidative stress.
Astringent: The astringent properties of littleleaf linden tea, due to tannins, help tighten gum tissues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate swelling.
Analgesic: Littleleaf linden tea contains compounds with analgesic effects, like flavonoids, which help manage gum swelling by alleviating pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of littleleaf linden tea, from active constituents such as volatile oils, help control gum swelling by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria in the oral cavity.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried littleleaf linden flowers.
Steep - Allow the herb to steep in the hot water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain - Strain the liquid from the flowers using a fine-mesh strainer.
Serve - Discard the used herb and drink the remaining tea while warm.
Repeat - Repeat this process up to three times a day for gum swelling relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, the leaves are used to make medicinal teas that help reduce gum inflammation.
Flowers: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, the flowers are used to soothe irritated gums and alleviate swelling.
Bark: High in tannins, the bark is used to create astringent teas that help decrease gum swelling.
Buds: Packed with antioxidants, the buds are used to make soothing teas that promote gum health and reduce swelling.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum swelling with herbal teas is to choose teas with antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties. Chamomile, sage, and clove teas are excellent options. Steep the tea bag in hot water for a few minutes, then let it cool down. Apply the tea bag directly to the affected area for 10-15 minutes. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day until the swelling reduces. Remember to maintain good oral hygiene, including brushing and flossing regularly. However, if the swelling persists or worsens, consult a dental professional.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum swelling that are treated with herbal teas include mouth ulcers, inflamed gums, and throat infections. Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and echinacea have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Chamomile tea, in particular, contains flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Drinking these herbal teas or using them as mouth rinses can provide relief from gum swelling and other mouth-related ailments. However, it is essential to consult a healthcare professional if symptoms persist.
